reasoning: Peer sheet is dense classic Denzi pixel-art sprites — characters and creatures with tight 1px dark outlines, precise pixel placement, high detail density, consistent internal shading with a defined light direction, and strong individual charm/personality. Candidate (vfx_heal_2_v01) is a large pixelated heart icon in green/gold with scattered white sparkle pixels. OUTLINE WEIGHT: peers all carry a crisp 1px dark perimeter outline; candidate has none — edges bleed directly into background. OUTLINE COLOR: no dedicated dark outline exists on candidate at all (fail). SHADOW DIRECTION: peers show consistent top-left or ambient shading; candidate's internal shading reads as gem-refraction with no clearly readable shadow direction. SHADOW COUNT: candidate layers multiple highlight/refraction zones inside the heart — peers use simpler, single-shadow treatment for each element. HIGHLIGHT INTENSITY: candidate is overlit — large bright-white pixel scatter and a prominent central highlight far exceeding the modest specular touches on peers. COLOR TEMPERATURE: green+gold palette is within peer range (greens and yellows exist in peers), so no hard fail here, but the particular cool-green dominance is atypical across the peer family. DETAIL DENSITY: at the displayed size the candidate is extremely sparse — chunky blocks with minimal pixel-level detail vs the densely painted peer characters; equivalent canvas area on peers contains 5-10x more distinct color decisions. PROPORTION: candidate is rendered at roughly 4-6x the native pixel footprint of peers even 'at matching scale', making direct style comparison unfavorable and suggesting different intended display resolution. RENDERING STYLE: peers use careful pixel-by-pixel Denzi character art; candidate reads as a stylized UI/icon asset with scatter-particle VFX treatment — a categorically different production pipeline. PALETTE OVERLAP: some overlap (greens, yellows) but candidate completely lacks the earthy browns, reds, purples, and blues common across the peer family. CHARM LEVEL: peers have characteristic Denzi charm — readable silhouettes with personality. Candidate heart is inert and generic, more consistent with mobile UI iconography than a Denzi sprite family. The style gap across 8 of 11 dimensions is too wide to pass or even reach marginal.
